description: Techniques and strategies tailored to different contract types, from partscores through grand slams, in both notrump and suit contracts.

hands.title: 301. Partscore play
hands.notes: In a partscore, just aim for 8 tricks. South has a comfortable 2S. Draw trumps carefully (cash A then lead toward KJ), establish your tricks, and don't take unnecessary risks. Making the partscore is the goal - overtricks are a bonus.

hands.title: 302. Game contract planning
hands.notes: In 4S, plan for 10 tricks at the start. Count winners: 5 spades, 1 heart, 4 diamonds = 10. Draw trumps, then cash diamonds (finesse the J if needed). Game contract planning means having a clear roadmap before playing card one.

hands.title: 303. Small slam technique
hands.notes: In 6S, South needs 12 tricks. Count: 5 spades, 2 hearts, 2 diamonds, 1 club = 10. Need 2 more. Ruff two diamonds in dummy after drawing trumps. In a small slam, minimize risk - take the safest line to 12 tricks even if it means foregoing a grand.

hands.title: 304. Grand slam technique
hands.notes: In 7S, South must take all 13 tricks. Count: 5 spades, 2 hearts, 3 diamonds (if they break 3-3), 1 club = 11. Need diamond break or ruffing plan. Draw trumps, test diamonds. In a grand slam, you cannot afford any safety play that concedes a trick.

hands.title: 305. Notrump partscore
hands.notes: In 1NT, South needs just 7 tricks. Count winners: 2 spades, 1 heart, 4 diamonds = 7. Establish diamonds first by leading toward the KJ. In notrump partscores, identify your longest combined suit and develop it immediately. No need for risky plays.

hands.title: 306. Notrump game
hands.notes: In 3NT, South needs 9 tricks. Diamonds provide 5 tricks. Add spade A, spade K (via finesse), heart A, club A = 9. Win the opening lead, knock out the diamond T, and run diamonds. In notrump game, establish your long suit before cashing winners elsewhere.

hands.title: 307. Major-suit game
hands.notes: In 4S, draw trumps (3 rounds), then develop diamonds for a discard. Major-suit games benefit from ruffing power. After trumps are drawn, play DK and DA, then ruff the fourth diamond if needed. The major-suit fit provides flexibility.

hands.title: 308. Minor-suit game
hands.notes: 5D requires 11 tricks - harder than 3NT. Count: 5 diamonds, 1 spade, 1 heart, 1 club = 8. Need 3 more from ruffs or establishment. Ruff two hearts in hand and establish a spade trick. Minor-suit games demand more tricks, so plan carefully.

hands.title: 309. Slam in trumps
hands.notes: In 6S, South needs 12 tricks. Count: 5 spades, 2 hearts, 2 diamonds, 1 club = 10. Ruff two clubs in dummy for tricks 11-12. In a trump slam, dummy's ruffing power is a key source of extra tricks. Draw only enough trumps to prevent overruffs.

hands.title: 310. Slam in notrump
hands.notes: In 6NT, South needs 12 tricks with no ruffing power. Count: 3 spades (finesse J), 2 hearts, 5 diamonds (if they break), 1 club = 11. Need the spade finesse or diamond break for the 12th trick. In notrump slams, every trick must come from high cards or long suits.
